nodes.label: Why Ozy Exists (Context Bloat Problem)
nodes.rationale: Connecting an agent to many MCP servers means stuffing every tool description into context, which does not scale and does not degrade gracefully when a server is offline. Ozy fixes that by acting as a single brokered entry point with a stable 3-tool interface, persistent catalog, hybrid search, and live-gated invocation.

nodes.label: Go Coding Conventions (errors, context, slog)
nodes.rationale: Idiomatic Go, gofmt/goimports; errors with %w and errors.Is/As at boundaries; respect context cancellation, no goroutine leaks; structured slog. Any change to the three MCP tools (findTool/describeTool/callTool) is a contract change and must update SPEC.md in the same change.

nodes.label: Ozy Product Thesis (configure once, index persistently)
nodes.rationale: Tool access should be configured once, indexed persistently, searched by capability, and exposed to agents through a minimal instructional interface. The agent should not need to see every available downstream tool at startup.
nodes.norm_label: ozy product thesis (configure once, index persistently)

nodes.label: Eleven Core Principles (§4)
nodes.rationale: Capability brokerage over proxying; one config source of truth; small stable agent surface (3 MVP tools); persistent searchable catalog; instructional responses; live-gated invocation; token economy as first-class quality; evaluation before confidence; adapter-neutral core; local-first and privacy-respecting; observable and diagnosable.

nodes.label: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF, k=60)
nodes.rationale: RRF is rank-based so BM25 lexical scores and cosine similarities from the sidecar need not be calibrated to a common scale. With no semantic signal, RRF over the single lexical list reduces to lexical order, so degraded path is identical to today's baseline. The use/ambiguous decision is made on the RRF gap between top two; no_good_match is gated on an absolute component floor (normalized lexical relevance OR cosine similarity) because RRF alone carries no absolute relevance.

nodes.label: agentInstruction Quality Criteria
nodes.rationale: Actionable: tell the agent the next concrete action. Conditional: distinguish retry/do-not-retry/ask-user/choose-alternative/refresh/report-failure cases. Grounded: base instructions only on known catalog/runtime/error state, do not imply successful execution, hidden retries, or unavailable capabilities.

nodes.label: Python Embedding Sidecar Architecture
nodes.rationale: Go owns runtime/daemon/MCP/CLI/catalog authority/online search/lexical BM25; Python sidecar owns the embedding model, embedding-metadata store (SQLite, toolRef <-> vector_id map, content hashes, model/version metadata, raw float32 vectors), and the ANN index. The sidecar does NOT own Ozy's authoritative catalog of tools, schemas, or runtime status. The vector index is a derived artifact, rebuildable from SQLite. Vector backend selection is fixed before the first index and immutable after.

nodes.label: Result Cache Configuration (cache.enabled/ttlSeconds/maxEntries)
nodes.rationale: Cache memoizes findTool/describeTool and read-only callTool results within TTL, keyed by content hash folded with the catalog generation (findTool) or the target tool's schemaHash (describe/call). callTool is cached only for tools whose downstream readOnlyHint is true; write tools and unknown intent always invoke live; failures are never cached.

nodes.label: Live-Gated Invocation Rule
nodes.rationale: Search and describe may be stale-tolerant. Calling tools is not. Ozy must not fabricate execution, silently call stale schemas, or hide downstream availability problems. If a downstream server is offline, schema drift is detected, or argument validation fails, Ozy must return structured, repair-oriented errors. No retry amplification.

nodes.label: Project Scaffold (single ozy binary, Go layout)
nodes.rationale: Buildable foundation: Go module -> single ozy executable via documented build command; cmd/ozy/ entrypoint and internal/ packages mapping to SPEC §6 architectural boundaries; working test + lint/vet targets with at least one baseline passing test so future changes inherit a functioning quality gate.
nodes.norm_label: project scaffold (single ozy binary, go layout)

nodes.label: Broker Result Cache Decorator
nodes.rationale: Transparent decorator over the shared Broker interface so both CLI and MCP adapter benefit without either path importing cache logic. Serves results for findTool/describeTool and read-only callTool within TTL. Default-deny for write tools: only callTool results for tools with readOnlyHint=true are cached; List is never cached. Cache key is a content hash of the request folded with the catalog generation (findTool) or the target tool's schemaHash (describe/call). Failures are never cached. Default-enabled; pass-through when disabled.

nodes.label: Tool Discovery via MCP tools/list
nodes.rationale: Ozy discovers tools of each connected downstream server by calling MCP tools/list, capturing name/title/description/input schema. Each tool is normalized to toolRef = <serverId>.<downstreamToolName>. ozy index populates the catalog and reports a summary of servers/tools indexed plus per-server failures. Each cataloged tool records lastIndexedAt, schemaHash, freshness marker, and server status.

nodes.label: Live Tool Invocation (callTool)
nodes.rationale: Resolve callTool toolRef to a configured downstream server and tool name by splitting on the first '.'; resolution uses Ozy's resolved configuration directly and does NOT require a prior ozy index. Ozy connects only to the one target server, calls downstream tools/call with a per-call timeout from server config, normalizes result into the §9.3 success shape, and maps downstream tool errors into structured §9.3 failures. Secrets in headers/environment are not leaked. Results respect budgets.callTool.maxResultBytes. No retry amplification.

nodes.label: Hybrid Tool Search (findTool)
nodes.rationale: Mandatory lexical relevance signal combined with optional semantic relevance signal fused by Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) over the persistent catalog. The semantic ranking is produced by the embedding sidecar. RRF is rank-based; no_good_match is gated on the underlying component scores (lexical relevance OR semantic similarity), not on RRF alone. Decisions: use / choose_from_candidates / known_but_unavailable / no_good_match / ambiguous / catalog_empty. findTool ranks tools from the persistent catalog even when downstream servers are unreachable, marking results with catalog freshness. Graceful degradation: returns a lexical-ranked decision when semantic is disabled, sidecar unavailable, or a query times out.

nodes.label: Ozy Uninstaller (plan-first, conservative)
nodes.rationale: Shared uninstaller between ozy uninstall and go run cmd/ozy-install@<version> uninstall. Plan-first: detects Ozy-managed locations, displays removal plan, asks before protected categories. Default removes installed binary + Ozy-managed runtime only; asks before config/logs/caches/models/vector stores/MCP definitions. Flags --keep-config, --keep-data exclude categories; --purge requires distinct explicit confirmation even if --yes is set. PATH/rc cleanup only removes the marked block the installer added and only with consent. Resumable state machine (DetectInstall, PlanRemovals, RemoveBinary, RemoveManagedData, CleanPath, WriteSummary). Durable, redacted log; safe-retry command on failure.

nodes.label: Pluggable Vector Storage Backends
nodes.rationale: turbovec is the zero-configuration default; FAISS is an opt-in alternative selected before the first index is built. Once an index exists, the backend is fixed — a configured/recorded mismatch forces a rebuild from SQLite rather than serving mixed artifacts. Vector backend supports facet-scoped nearest-neighbor search via an allowlist of vector ids derived from the SQLite facet filter; results are not over-fetched and post-filtered. Persisted index survives restart, fully rebuildable from SQLite without re-embedding; load verifies backend/model/dimension match before serving.

nodes.label: SQLite Embedding Store (source of truth for vectors)
nodes.rationale: store.py (SQLite) is the source of truth for embeddings: toolRef <-> vector_id, content hashes, facets, active backend/model/dim. The vector index is a derived artifact that can always be rebuilt from SQLite without re-embedding. The active backend is recorded in SQLite and is immutable after the first index.
nodes.norm_label: sqlite embedding store (source of truth for vectors)

nodes.label: Go Sidecar Client (one Client = one process)
nodes.norm_label: go sidecar client (one client = one process)

nodes.label: JSONL Wire Protocol (health/upsert/delete/query/stats)
nodes.rationale: One JSON object per line over stdin/stdout. Operations: health, upsert, delete, query, stats. One in-flight request at a time; reader goroutine dispatches by id. Any failure (timeout, crash, garbage response, provisioning failure) marks the client unavailable without panicking — search falls back to the lexical baseline.

nodes.label: On-demand venv Provisioning (uv + pip fallback)
nodes.rationale: On-demand venv provisioning via uv with python -m venv + pip fallback. Provisioning never blocks daemon readiness: any failure (no uv, no python3, pip install error) leaves the daemon ready in lexical-only mode with a surfaced notice.

nodes.label: installer matrix job (ubuntu/macos/windows)
nodes.rationale: The installer's PATH edits, shell rc handling, and filesystem/permission behavior are platform-specific, so the installer + paths packages run natively on each OS. Linux covers the state machine, dependency mocks, non-TTY/dry-run, and retry paths; macOS and Windows add the native PATH/rc/filesystem coverage. No real installs run — every test uses fakes.
